question: Design a `Department` class that maintains a list of `Employee` objects (aggregation). The `Employee` class should have `name` and `id`. The `Department` class should have `name` and methods to `addEmployee` and `listEmployees`. Demonstrate how employees can exist independently of the department (e.g., an employee can be added to multiple departments or removed from one without being destroyed).

test_cases.expected:
--- Demonstrating Correct Aggregation with std::shared_ptr ---
Employee created: Alice (ID: 101)
Employee created: Bob (ID: 102)
Employee created: Charlie (ID: 103)

--- Creating Departments ---
Department created: Human Resources
Department created: IT Department
Department created: Sales

--- Adding Employees to Departments ---
  Added Alice to Human Resources
  Added Bob to Human Resources
  Added Alice to IT Department
  Added Charlie to IT Department
  Added Bob to Sales

--- Initial Department Listings ---
Department: Human Resources
  Employee ID: 101, Name: Alice
  Employee ID: 102, Name: Bob
Department: IT Department
  Employee ID: 101, Name: Alice
  Employee ID: 103, Name: Charlie
Department: Sales
  Employee ID: 102, Name: Bob

--- Demonstrating Independence (Employee Alice is in HR and IT) ---
Removing Alice from HR Department (employee ID 101)...
  Removed employee with ID 101 from Human Resources

--- Department Listings After Alice's Removal from HR ---
Department: Human Resources
  Employee ID: 102, Name: Bob
Department: IT Department
  Employee ID: 101, Name: Alice
  Employee ID: 103, Name: Charlie
Department: Sales
  Employee ID: 102, Name: Bob

--- End of main scope: Departments and Employees will be destroyed ---
Department destroyed: Sales
Department destroyed: IT Department
Department destroyed: Human Resources
Employee destroyed: Charlie (ID: 103)
Employee destroyed: Bob (ID: 102)
Employee destroyed: Alice (ID: 101)
